facts.text: MacDonnell Heights sits at 41°42′59″N 73°51′38″W, 207 ft above sea level, a compact 0.63 sq mi community in Dutchess County.
Route 44, the Dutchess Turnpike, cuts through the heart of MacDonnell Heights, linking it directly to downtown Poughkeepsie just 4 miles west.
The area’s early growth was spurred by IBM’s 1941 campus in nearby Poughkeepsie, whose 1950s and 1960s expansion brought high‑tech jobs and a residential boom to MacDonnell Heights.
Wappinger Creek borders MacDonnell Heights to the southeast, a waterway that historically powered mills and drained the surrounding lowlands.
MacDonnell Heights’ population is largely White (72.3%) with notable Black, Hispanic, and Asian minorities, reflecting a diverse community profile.
The hamlets of MacDonnell Heights and Colonial Heights form the core residential area, sharing the same CDP designation and community identity.
